title: Precision Cancer Drugs and Combos: Troy Wilson on The Long Run - TimmermanReport.com
gist: Kura Oncology won FDA approval for menin inhibitor ziftomenib (Komzifti) in AML in Nov 2025 and is betting a second drug, farnesyl transferase inhibitor darlifarnib, revives a written-off drug class as a logical complement to Revolution Medicines' RAS inhibitor daraxonrasib.

insights.claim: Kura Oncology's menin inhibitor ziftomenib (brand Komzifti) was FDA-approved in November 2025 as an oral pill for acute myeloid leukemia patients with specific genetic alterations such as NPM1 mutations or KMT2A rearrangements.
insights.speaker: Timmerman Report
insights.so_what: A de novo FDA-approved asset from a 2014 startup validates Kura's platform and gives a commercial anchor; watch label-expansion trials into broader AML.
insights.quote: Ziftomenib was approved by the FDA in November 2025, initially for a small set of patients with acute myeloid leukemia who have specific genetic alterations such as NPM1 mutations or KMT2A rearrangements.

insights.claim: Kura frames its current AML approval as only the beginning and intends to expand ziftomenib into a broader group of AML patients, a notoriously difficult-to-treat cancer.
insights.speaker: Timmerman Report
insights.so_what: Upside case hinges on label expansion beyond the initial narrow genetic subset; broader AML indications would materially enlarge the addressable market.

themes.theme: FTI comeback and RAS combo thesis

insights.claim: Kura's second drug darlifarnib is a farnesyl transferase inhibitor, a class once dismissed as a pharma graveyard that Kura scientists believe is due for a comeback.
insights.speaker: Timmerman Report
insights.so_what: A contrarian bet on reviving a failed drug class; a second approval would de-risk Kura as a repeatable developer rather than a one-drug story.
insights.quote: this entire class of FTI medicines – once written off as a pharma graveyard – is due for a comeback
insights.locator: Kura is also hopeful lightning will hit twice

insights.claim: Kura argues FTI drugs like darlifarnib should logically complement the new RAS inhibitor daraxonrasib from Revolution Medicines, implying a combination-therapy rationale tied to RAS pathway biology.
insights.speaker: Timmerman Report
insights.so_what: Positions darlifarnib as a combo partner riding the RAS-inhibitor wave; watch for Kura-Revolution Medicines combination data as a value catalyst.
insights.quote: it logically ought to complement the new breakthrough RAS inhibitor, daraxonrasib, from Revolution Medicines

insights.claim: Kura CEO Troy Wilson previously co-founded three companies all acquired by large pharma: Intellikine (PI3-kinase inhibitors, bought by Takeda), Ambrx (antibody-drug conjugates, bought by Johnson & Johnson), and Avidity Biosciences (antibody oligonucleotide conjugates for rare muscular diseases, bought by Novartis).
insights.speaker: Timmerman Report
insights.so_what: A serial founder with three prior successful exits raises the base rate that Kura ends in acquisition; a signal for M&A-oriented biotech investors.
insights.quote: He co-founded three previous companies that ended up being successfully acquired
insights.locator: Troy arrives at this auspicious moment

insights.claim: Reaching a self-developed FDA-approved medicine all the way through R&D is rare for a biotech startup, a bar Kura has now cleared.
insights.speaker: Timmerman Report
insights.so_what: Crossing the full R&D gauntlet distinguishes Kura from the many clinical-stage names that never reach commercialization.
insights.quote: Not many biotech startup companies make it all the way through the R&D gauntlet to develop an FDA approved medicine.

themes.theme: AI and pricing disruption in drug development (sponsor-derived)

insights.claim: Sponsor commentary asserts AI has moved from pilot programs into real biopharma workflows and that AI execution is becoming a key competitive differentiator in drug development.
insights.speaker: Timmerman Report
insights.so_what: Reflects the framing that AI capability is shifting from experiment to operational moat in pharma; treat as sponsor marketing, not independent analysis.
insights.quote: AI has advanced from pilot programs into real biopharma workflows.
insights.locator: The Long Run is sponsored by

insights.claim: Bioanalysis startup Dash Bio publishes CRO pricing publicly with an online calculator, challenging an industry norm where pricing is treated as a secret requiring NDAs, scoping calls, and week-long Scope of Work turnaround.
insights.speaker: Timmerman Report
insights.so_what: A transparency-and-speed play in the opaque CRO/bioanalysis market; watch whether public pricing pressures incumbent CRO margins.
insights.quote: In bioanalysis, pricing gets treated like a closely-guarded secret.
insights.locator: Quick question: when was the last time
